passage.title: Play Choice Board
passage.text: Our group keeps a toy turn chart beside the rug. During the first break, Hana checks it before music class. The toy turn chart has small pictures for indoor games and art trays for the day's job. Mr. Park asks us to choose one game area and wait our turn. Sometimes we compare today's picture with yesterday's picture and notice one small change. We like the toy turn chart because it helps us keep the game area neat.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the toy turn chart is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the toy turn chart?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the toy turn chart is beside the rug.

questions.prompt: When does Hana use the toy turn chart?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Hana uses it during the first break.

questions.prompt: What does the toy turn chart have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the toy turn chart has game pictures.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
